comment: GENERIC CLOCK RULE: no absolute dates in mock data. expires_in_days is a day offset from
'today' (IClock.Today — real date, or simulated via control set_date/advance_day). The
adapter resolves offsets to ISO dates at read time. null = non-perishable.
v3.4: this one file feeds three of the five demo goals, so the numbers are chosen to make
each one true at the same time —
meal_plan / waste : spinach, yogurt, bread and chicken expire within 3 days, so a week
of dinners genuinely has something to rescue.
grocery_cost      : milk sits BELOW its restock_threshold (0.5 of 1 L) and eggs are at
theirs, which is what grocery.json's day-4 stock-out refers to.
vacation_prep     : the same short-dated perishables are what must be eaten or frozen
restock_threshold must be in the SAME UNIT as quantity — InventorySuggester compares them
raw, so a threshold in grams against a quantity in kg would fire nonsense.
